title: Mercator Ocean - Ocean State Report 4 summary
organisation: Mercator Ocean, the French center for analysis and forecasting of the global ocean
summary: We worked with Mercator Ocean on the communication strategy for its annual Ocean State Report.
description: The report, based on data from the European Copernicus Earth Observation Programme, provides comprehensive state-of-the-art information on the ongoing changes and natural variations in the global ocean and European regional seas.
production_credit: Complete Production of "Mercator Ocean - Ocean State Report 4 summary": Design & Data GmbH

title: Mercator Ocean - SROCC social media video
organisation: Copernicus Marine Environment Monitoring Service (CMEMS) managed by Mercator Ocean
summary: The Copernicus Marine Environment Monitoring Service (CMEMS) managed by Mercator Ocean provides full, free and open access to data & information related to our ocean.
description: Following the release of the Special Report on the Ocean and Cryosphere (SROCC) by the Intergovernmental Panel on Climate Change (IPCC), we were commissioned to produce a social media video to demonstrate the use of Copernicus data and how it provides vital information needed to adapt to the changing ocean environment.
production_credit: Complete Production of "Mercator Ocean - SROCC social media video": Design & Data GmbH

title: Copernicus ECMWF - CAMS Windy
organisation: European Centre for Medium-Range Weather Forecasts (ECMWF)
summary: Design & Data worked with Copernicus ECMWF to communicate case studies, which demonstrate the impact and benefits of Copernicus for service users and European citizens.
description: Windy is a leading Czech start-up that enables approximately 800 000 people per day to see information about different weather parameters laid over a world map. It provides global air quality information provided by the Copernicus Atmosphere Monitoring Service (CAMS) implemented by the European Centre for Medium-Range Weather Forecasts (ECMWF) on behalf of the European Union.
production_credit: Complete Production of "Copernicus ECMWF - CAMS Windy": Design & Data GmbH

title: ESA - PhiWeek Bootcamp 2019
organisation: European Space Agency
summary: Organised by Design & Data, the PhiWeek Bootcamp was hosted in 2019 by the European Space Agency (ESA) in the framework of Phi-Week.
description: It is was intensive 5-day event, guiding you through the design sprint process and answering critical questions in the prototyping and testing of ideas relating to Earth Observation.
production_credit: Complete Production of "ESA - PhiWeek Bootcamp 2019": Design & Data GmbH

title: Copernicus ECMWF - CAMS Breezometer
organisation: European Centre for Medium-Range Weather Forecasts (ECMWF)
summary: Design & Data worked with Copernicus ECMWF to communicate case studies, which demonstrate the impact and benefits of Copernicus for service users and European citizens.
description: Breezometer provides a source for air quality data, enabling individuals to make better health decisions based on the air pollution levels at their specific location and individual sensitivities. It uses air quality information provided by the Copernicus Atmosphere Monitoring Service (CAMS) implemented by the European Centre for Medium-Range Weather Forecasts (ECMWF) on behalf of the European Union.
production_credit: Complete Production of "Copernicus ECMWF - CAMS Breezometer": Design & Data GmbH

title: ECMWF - Vortex - European State of the Climate event 10.04.2018
organisation: European Centre for Medium-Range Weather Forecasts (ECMWF)
summary: A series of videos highlighting different use cases were shown to the European Parliament at the European State of the Climate event on 10 April 2018 to illustrate the important role data from Copernicus services implemented by ECMWF: CAMS and C3S.
description: Vortex use data from C3S for its online wind modelling service. Vortex provides tools for wind farm development, among others that enable critical decision-making and easy-to-use solutions.
production_credit: Complete Production: Design & Data GmbH

title: ECMWF - DiscovAir app - European State of the Climate event 10.04.2018
organisation: European Centre for Medium-Range Weather Forecasts (ECMWF)
summary: A series of videos highlighting different use cases were shown to the European Parliament at the European State of the Climate event on 10 April 2018 to illustrate the important role data from Copernicus services implemented by ECMWF: CAMS and C3S.
description: DiscovAir use data from CAMS to help tourists prepare appropriately to enjoy their experience in Greece and Cyprus. In combination with personal health information, DiscovAir recommends an optimal time to visit any given location, based on its air quality, UV index, pollen, and temperature available. An app will be available to the public shortly and is currently being evaluated by beta-testers.
production_credit: Complete Production: Design & Data GmbH

title: ECMWF - Riga airTEXT - European State of the Climate event 10.04.2018
organisation: European Centre for Medium-Range Weather Forecasts (ECMWF)
summary: A series of videos highlighting different use cases were shown to the European Parliament at the European State of the Climate event on 10 April 2018 to illustrate the important role data from Copernicus services implemented by ECMWF: CAMS and C3S.
description: Riga airTEXT use CAMS data to provide air quality monitoring to Latvian residents. Users can track air quality, UV, pollen and temperature forecasts across the city of Riga online or through text alerts.
production_credit: Complete Production: Design & Data GmbH

title: ECMWF - Copernicus services implemented by ECMWF - European State of the Climate event 10.04.2018
organisation: European Centre for Medium-Range Weather Forecasts (ECMWF)
summary: The ECMWF operates the Copernicus Atmosphere Monitoring Service (CAMS) and the Copernicus Climate Change Service (C3S) on behalf of the EU.
description: Presented to the European Parliament at the European State of the Climate event on 10 April 2018, this video presents how these services offer a way to better understand our planet, protect and sustain our environment and respond to climate change and challenges in key sectors.
production_credit: Complete Production: Design & Data GmbH

title: ECMWF - Copernicus ECMWF Overview - European State of the Climate event 10.04.2018
organisation: European Centre for Medium-Range Weather Forecasts (ECMWF)
summary: The ECMWF operates the Copernicus Atmosphere Monitoring Service (CAMS) and the Copernicus Climate Change Service (C3S) on behalf of the EU.
description: Presented to the European Parliament at the European State of the Climate event on 10 April 2018, the video demonstrates how with decades of experience in delivering weather and climate modelling, ECMWF have the expertise, facilities, and structure in place to process and make raw Copernicus data accessible to Europe’s scientific, business and policy sectors.
production_credit: Complete Production: Design & Data GmbH

title: ECMWF - Trailer - European State of the Climate event 10.04.2018
organisation: European Centre for Medium-Range Weather Forecasts (ECMWF)
summary: On the 10th April 2018, the Copernicus services implemented by ECMWF were presented at a high-level event under the patronage of the European Parliament.
description: The aim of the event was to raise awareness and support of the Copernicus Programme among MEPs and showcase the European State of the Climate report. This trailer was shown to introduce the event.
production_credit: Complete Production:: Design & Data GmbH

title: ECMWF - Background Slideshow - European State of the Climate event 10.04.2018
organisation: European Centre for Medium-Range Weather Forecasts (ECMWF)
summary: On the 10th April 2018, the Copernicus services implemented by ECMWF were presented at a high-level event under the patronage of the European Parliament.
description: The ECMWF operates the Copernicus Atmosphere Monitoring Service (CAMS) and the Copernicus Climate Change Service (C3S) on behalf of the EU. This slideshow was presented to MEPs to illustrate the benefits of Copernicus’ freely accessible environmental data which help us to better understand our planet and inform us in our decision-making. The slideshow is without audio.
production_credit: Complete Production:: Design & Data GmbH

title: DLR - INNOspace Weekend 2017 Event Video
organisation: DLR Space Administration
summary: Event video for the INNOspace Weekend 2017, a new event hosted by the DLR Space Administration.
description: In the course of the INNOspace initiative the event was realised as a 3-day intensive weekend of activities where aspiring entrepreneurs could unlock their creative potential, invent the future and build a startup in just 60 hours.
production_credit: Complete production by Design & Data GmbH for DLR Space Administration.

title: DLR - INNOspace Weekend Teaser
organisation: DLR - Deutsches Zentrum für Luft- und Raumfahrt e.V.
summary: 30 second trailer for the INNOspace Weekend, a new event hosted by the DLR Space Administration.
description: In the course of the INNOspace initiative is a 3-day intensive weekend of activities where aspiring entrepreneurs could unlock their creative potential, invent the future and build a startup in just 60 hours.
production_credit: Animations, design and production by Design & Data GmbH for DLR - Deutsches Zentrum für Luft- und Raumfahrt e.V..

title: ESA - Living with space 4.0
organisation: ESA - European Space Agency
summary: Space technology surrounds us, sometimes without us even noticing – from the moment we wake up to when we sleep at night.
description: Every day space benefits us all, depicted in our latest video.
production_credit: Animations, design, storyboard and production by Design & Data GmbH for ESA- European Space Agency.

title: EUMETSAT and Africa: An Overview
organisation: EUMETSAT
summary: In the first of a series of five videos showcasing EUMETSAT's activities in Africa, we get an overview of their work in the region.
description: We explore how EUMETSAT supports the African community to make the best use of meteorological satellite data through a number of projects and initiatives in cooperation with the European Union, the African Union and the World Meteorological Organization. These in turn enable African meteorologists to give severe weather warnings to local communities, enable them to manage their water and agriculture, and help mitigate the effects of natural hazards and climate change.
production_credit: Complete Production of "EUMETSAT and Africa - An Overview": Design & Data GmbH

title: EUMETSAT and Africa: Food Security
organisation: EUMETSAT
summary: In the second of a series of five videos showcasing EUMETSAT's activities in Africa, we look at the importance of food security in the region.
description: We see how satellite data provided by EUMETSAT is important for agriculture and fisheries in Africa (e.g. land surface, rainfall, vegetation, evapo-transpiration, soil moisture, sea surface temperature and ocean colour). In combination with ground observations, users can provide vital information to the policymakers in agriculture and fisheries, reinforcing food security in Africa.
production_credit: Complete Production of "EUMETSAT and Africa - Food Security": Design & Data GmbH

title: Rosetta Strato Charity Campaign
organisation: Design & Data GmbH with Universe Awareness (UNAWE)
summary: We want to help continue Rosetta's legacy, by sharing her heroic story, using the plush toy as an interactive teaching resource to educate school children around the world in astronomy and space sciences.
description: Design & Data GmbH has partnered with Universe Awareness (UNAWE) to help train teachers and send them one of these lovely toys for use in the classroom. With your pledge we will send a toy to where it's needed most: underprivileged schools around the world. We hope we can count on your support! Your pledge can enter you into a raffle to win the unique Rosetta Strato toy that ventured to the stratosphere.
production_credit: Production: Design & Data GmbH

title: ESA - Making and testing for Mercury
organisation: ESA - European Space Agency
summary: The ESA-JAXA BepiColombo mission to Mercury must withstand extremely harsh conditions while in orbit around the innermost planet of our Solar System.
production_credit: Complete production of "Making and testing for Mercury": Design & Data GmbH

title: ESA - Securing Aeolus against atomic oxygen
organisation: ESA - European Space Agency
summary: Low-orbiting space missions can be susceptible to atomic oxygen erosion.
description: ESA’s Materials and Electrical Components Laboratory was tasked with investigating the resilience of beta cloth fibre.
production_credit: Complete production of "Securing Aelous Against Atomic Oxygen": Design & Data GmbH

title: ESA - Securing Aeolus against atomic oxygen - Subtitled
summary: Low-orbiting space missions can be susceptible to atomic oxygen erosion.
description: ESA’s Materials and Electrical Components Laboratory was tasked with investigating the resilience of beta cloth fibre.

title: ESA - Getting Vega’s launch tower moving
organisation: ESA - European Space Agency
summary: Once its mobile launch gantry is moved away, then Europe’s Vega launcher is primed for lift-off – except for the time the stuck gantry refused to move.
description: Solving the problem and getting the gantry mobile again was one of the more unusual tasks of ESA’s Materials and Electrical Components Laboratory.
production_credit: Complete production of "Getting Vega's launch tower moving": Design & Data GmbH

title: Rosetta Plush Toy Reaches Stratosphere
summary: We asked ourselves, could the Rosetta toy also survive the harsh conditions of space?
description: Every space mission involves a rigorous period of modelling and testing; the Rosetta Strato mission was no different. Our aim was to recreate a key scene from the Rosetta cartoon series with the toy’s solar panels deployed. There was every risk however this might not work. On 17th March 2016 at 10:40 CET from Gniezno in Poland we decided to launch. With steady conditions and clear, blue skies we released the toy attached to a stratospheric balloon where it reached over 30km in altitude. But could we capture the key scene of the solar panels deployed? And could we recover the toy? Following a short search, the toy was successfully recovered from its space adventure; it landed on a tree-shaped comet near Miąskowo, Poland. We’re very happy to finally reveal the footage of its journey to the stratosphere and back to Earth. Enjoy!
credits: Many thanks to Blue Dot Solutions for their technical expertise in helping to make this mission a success. Music: Dvořák Symphony No. 9 III performed by Colombia University Orchestra. Production by Design & Data GmbH.
